message:
fix(ci): fresh-clone workflow correctly detects partial sibling presence

The earlier guard used `ls -d dir1 dir2 dir3 2>/dev/null`, relying on the
exit code to decide whether any sibling repos were present. GNU `ls` with
a mix of existing and missing arguments returns exit 2 — identical to
the all-missing case — so the check couldn't distinguish "no siblings"
from "some siblings." A partial-presence runner would pass the guard
silently even though resolution would cut through a local copy and miss
the #181 regression.

Loop with `[ -e "../$sibling" ]` per sibling instead. Also pin `set
-euo pipefail` in this step so future shell errors don't sneak through.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -76,9 +76,16 @@ jobs:
       - name: cargo metadata (full resolution, no siblings present)
         working-directory: cass
         run: |
-          # Confirm `ls ..` has no frankensqlite/frankensearch/franken_agent_detection
-          if ls -d ../frankensqlite ../frankensearch ../franken_agent_detection 2>/dev/null; then
-            echo "Unexpected sibling repos present — test invalid."
-            exit 2
-          fi
+          set -euo pipefail
+          # Confirm no sibling repo is present — otherwise we'd resolve through
+          # the user's local copy and miss the regression we're trying to catch.
+          # Check each sibling separately (GNU `ls -d a b c` returns non-zero
+          # when any argument doesn't exist, so a mix of present/missing looks
+          # the same exit-code-wise as all-missing — not a usable discriminator).
+          for sibling in frankensqlite frankensearch franken_agent_detection; do
+            if [ -e "../$sibling" ]; then
+              echo "Unexpected sibling ../$sibling is present — test invalid."
+              exit 2
+            fi
+          done
           cargo metadata --format-version 1 > /dev/null
